hero.label: Made for VetRec
hero.headline: I want to build VetRec's first sales engine.
hero.subheadline: VetRec is solving a real problem for veterinarians by automating the administrative burden that keeps them from patient care. I've spent the last year learning how to identify, qualify, and close early adopters in B2B, and I'm ready to apply that playbook to a product I genuinely believe in during the critical growth phase.

whyCompany.title: Solving a mission-driven problem in healthcare
whyCompany.body: Veterinarians are drowning in SOAP notes and administrative work. VetRec's AI scribe directly improves patient care and practice efficiency, not just revenue. That's a product I can authentically sell because it creates real value, not just another feature.

whyCompany.title: Y Combinator S23 company at inflection point
whyCompany.body: VetRec is 25 people, founder-led by Kevin Cohen (ex-Microsoft), and hiring for SDRs now. This is the moment where early sales execution defines whether they hit escape velocity. I want to be part of building that foundation.

whyCompany.title: My BDR experience directly translates here
whyCompany.body: At Copygen, I ran cold outreach and LinkedIn sequences that converted into 15-20 paying clients. VetRec needs someone who can identify veterinary decision makers, qualify pain around administrative burden, and create pipeline. That's exactly what I've done, just with a different buyer persona.

quote: I spent the last year learning what it takes to move deals forward in B2B. Now I want to do it for a company where better software actually means better patient care.
